name: Static gravitational slip prediction
hypothesis: TAUG predicts gravitational slip eta = Phi/Psi = 23/17 ≈ 1.353 in the late-universe (z~0 to z~2) quasi-static, scale-independent regime, derived from DHOST-Ia parameters (alphaH=1/8, alphaT=0, alphaM=3/16, beta1=-1/16, Lambda=17/16) via the formula eta = 1 + 2*alphaM/Lambda.

falsification_criteria.name: Euclid DR1 expected precision (Oct 2026)
falsification_criteria.rejection_logic: If σ_Euclid < 0.10 and central remains within 1σ of zero, TAUG H1 is rejected at >3σ

known_issues: Formula eta = 1 + 2*alphaM/Lambda not verbatim found in LMNV17 (1703.03797) sections 3-4
LMNV17 only provides Newtonian-limit formula Psi = (1+alphaH)/(1+alphaT) Phi which gives eta = 8/9 for TAUG (different sign!)
Bellini-Sawicki 2014 provides QSA cosmological formula but with different definition (eta_BS = 2 Psi/(Psi+Phi)) and more parameters
5 alternative formulas for TAUG yield eta in [1.20, 1.41]
